steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows a comma after a complete independent clause (“the Pyramid of Userkaf is one of the oldest known structures in the world”). A comma after an independent clause can introduce a participial phrase that modifies the subject of that clause — the Pyramid of Userkaf. The blank needs a present participle, not a finite verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — to provide: an infinitive phrase. Infinitives after a comma can express purpose, but they would need a clearer causal link here; more importantly, they do not form a modifying participial phrase. ✗
  • Choice B — provides: a finite present-tense verb. Adding a second finite verb after the comma creates a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — provide: a bare base-form verb. This is neither a participle nor a finite verb with a subject, so it is grammatically incomplete. ✗
  • Choice D — providing: a present participle. “Providing researchers a glimpse into ages long gone” is a participial phrase correctly modifying the Pyramid. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
